3 min read
6 hours ago

--

The weather forecast promised sunshine.
Instead, I woke to the steady rhythm of rain tapping against the roof of the RV.
Some campers see a rainy day as a disappointment. I see it as an opportunity.
After making a fresh cup of coffee, I settle into a cozy nook with my cats, open my laptop, and let the sound of the rain become the day’s soundtrack. There’s something about being parked in a quiet campground that makes it easier to focus than any home office ever could.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

coding with good companyMy Office Has Better Views
Working in technology means spending plenty of time behind a screen. Whether I’m designing web pages, troubleshooting an e-commerce issue, or learning a new programming language, most days happen indoors.
The RV changes that.
One glance out the window replaces the city with towering trees, mist rolling through the campground, or a lake disappearing into the morning fog. The scenery doesn’t distract me — it helps me concentrate.
It’s amazing what a different view can do for creative thinking.
Rain Makes Great Background Music
